description: The `created_at` column in the `customers` table stores the non-nullable timestamp indicating when a customer record was initially created.
Unique identifier for each customer in the retail operations system.
Stores the email address of the customer, allowing null values, with a maximum length of 450 characters.
Stores the given name of a customer as a non-null, case-insensitive Unicode string.
The "last_name" column in the "customers" table stores the non-nullable family name of a customer as a case-insensitive Unicode string.
Stores the customer's phone number as a nullable text field for contact purposes.
The `updated_at` column in the `customers` table stores the non-nullable timestamp of the most recent update to a customer's record.
The `created_at` column records the non-nullable timestamp indicating when an employee record was initially created in the system.
The "email" column in the "employees" table stores the unique, non-null email address of each employee for contact purposes.
Unique identifier for each employee in the retail operations system.
The "first_name" column in the "employees" table stores the non-nullable given name of an employee as a Unicode string.
The `hire_date` column in the `employees` table stores the non-nullable date an employee was hired, used for tracking employment start dates.
The "last_name" column in the "employees" table stores the non-nullable family name of an employee as a case-insensitive Unicode string.
Indicates the job position or function of an employee within the organization, stored as a non-nullable text value.
Identifies the store where the employee is assigned, referencing the `store_id` column in the `stores` table.
Tracks the timestamp of the most recent update to an employee record, ensuring accurate audit and change tracking.
The `created_at` column records the non-nullable timestamp indicating when the inventory record was initially created.
Unique identifier for each inventory record, serving as the primary key for the inventory table.
The `last_restocked_at` column records the timestamp of the most recent inventory restocking event, allowing null values if no restocking has occurred.
References the unique identifier of a product from the products table, linking inventory records to specific products.
The `quantity_on_hand` column in the `inventory` table stores the current stock level of a product in integer format and cannot be null.
Indicates the minimum quantity of a product in inventory that triggers a reorder, stored as a non-null integer.
Current stock unit label.
Represents the monetary value of inventory stock, stored as a numeric value with up to two decimal places, and may be null.
Identifier for the store associated with the inventory record, referencing the `store_id` column in the `stores` table.
The `updated_at` column in the `inventory` table stores the non-nullable timestamp of the most recent update to an inventory record.
Indicates whether a product is active and available for transactions, stored as a non-nullable boolean value.
Indicates the product's category classification, such as "Books," "Clothing," or "Electronics," stored as a non-nullable nvarchar string.
The `cost_price` column stores the non-null numeric cost amount (up to 10 digits with 2 decimal places) representing the purchase price of a product in the retail system.
Indicates the timestamp when the product record was initially created, stored as a non-nullable datetime value.
Source length unit (cm/in) used for unit inference testing.
Stores the length measurement of a product as a numeric value with up to 10 digits and 2 decimal places, nullable.
The "name" column in the "products" table stores the non-nullable name of each product as a Unicode string.
Primary supplier relationship used for join candidate detection.
Stores an optional textual description of the product.
Unique identifier for each product in the retail system, serving as the primary key for the products table.
Unique alphanumeric identifier for a product used for inventory and sales tracking, stored as a non-nullable nvarchar(450).
Identifier linking each product to its supplier, referencing the `supplier_id` column in the `suppliers` table.
Represents the retail price of a product in the store, stored as a non-null numeric value with up to 10 digits and 2 decimal places.
The `updated_at` column stores the non-nullable timestamp of the most recent update to a product record in the `products` table.
Source weight unit (kg/lb) used for unit inference testing.
The `weight_value` column stores the weight of a product as a numeric value with up to 10 digits and 2 decimal places, and it is nullable.
Approver employee foreign key for procurement workflow.
The `created_at` column records the timestamp when a purchase order entry was initially created in the system and cannot be null.
The "expected_date" column in the "purchase_orders" table records the anticipated delivery date of a purchase order, allowing null values.
The `order_date` column in the `purchase_orders` table stores the non-null date when a purchase order was placed.
Unique identifier for purchase orders in the retail operations system.
Indicates the current state of a purchase order (e.g., "Ordered", "Received") as a non-null categorical value.
Identifies the store associated with a purchase order, referencing the `store_id` column in the `stores` table.
The `supplier_id` column in the `purchase_orders` table is a non-nullable foreign key referencing `suppliers.supplier_id`, identifying the supplier associated with each purchase order.
The `updated_at` column records the timestamp of the most recent update to a purchase order and cannot be null.
Records the timestamp when a purchase order item entry is created, stored as a non-nullable datetime value.
Ordered quantity unit (ea/box).
The `ordered_qty_value` column stores the numeric quantity value (up to two decimal places) of items ordered in a purchase order, which can be null.
Links each purchase order item to its corresponding purchase order in the `purchase_orders` table via a non-null foreign key.
Unique identifier for each purchase order item record in the `purchase_order_items` table.
Identifier linking each purchase order item to a specific product in the products table.
The `quantity` column in the `purchase_order_items` table stores the non-null integer value representing the number of units of a product included in a specific purchase order item.
Represents the per-unit cost of a product in a purchase order, stored as a non-null numeric value with up to 10 digits and 2 decimal places.
Records the timestamp of the most recent update to a purchase order item, ensuring accurate tracking of modifications.
The `created_at` column in the `sales_orders` table stores the non-nullable timestamp indicating when the sales order record was initially created.
Represents the identifier of the customer associated with a sales order, referencing the `customer_id` column in the `customers` table, and may be null if no customer is linked.
Identifies the employee responsible for processing the sales order, referencing the `employees.employee_id` column.
The `order_date` column in the `sales_orders` table stores the non-nullable timestamp indicating when a sales order was placed.
Unique identifier for each sales order in the system.
Sales representative foreign key for order ownership.
Indicates the current state of a sales order, such as 'Pending' or 'Completed', stored as a non-nullable text category.
Identifies the store associated with a sales order, referencing the `store_id` column in the `stores` table.
The `total_amount` column in the `sales_orders` table stores the non-nullable total monetary value of a sales order as a numeric value with up to 12 digits and 2 decimal places.
The `updated_at` column records the timestamp of the most recent update to a sales order, ensuring accurate tracking of modifications, and it is mandatory.
The `created_at` column records the timestamp when a sales order item was created, stored as a non-nullable `datetime2` value.
Identifier for the product associated with the sales order item, referencing the primary key in the products table.
The "quantity" column in the "sales_order_items" table stores the non-null integer value representing the number of units of a product included in a specific sales order item.
References the unique identifier of the associated sales order in the `sales_orders` table, establishing a relationship between sales order items and their parent sales orders.
Unique identifier for each item in a sales order, serving as the primary key for the `sales_order_items` table.
Sold quantity unit (ea/box).
Represents the numeric value of the quantity sold for a sales order item, allowing up to two decimal places and nullable for cases where the quantity is not recorded.
The `unit_price` column in the `sales_order_items` table stores the per-unit selling price of a product in the sales order, represented as a non-nullable numeric value with two decimal places.
The `updated_at` column stores the non-nullable timestamp of the most recent update to a sales order item record for tracking modification history.
The "address" column in the "stores" table stores the street address of a retail store location as a nullable Unicode string.
The "city" column in the "stores" table stores the name of the city where a store is located, allowing null values.
Unique alphanumeric identifier for each store, used for internal reference and operations.
The `created_at` column stores the non-null timestamp indicating when a store record was initially created in the system.
The "name" column in the "stores" table is a non-nullable nvarchar field intended to store the name of a retail store.
The "phone" column in the "stores" table stores the contact phone number of a store as a nullable nvarchar value.
The "postal_code" column in the "stores" table stores the postal code of a store location as a nullable nvarchar value.
Indicates the state or province where the store is located, stored as a nullable nvarchar value.
Unique identifier for each store in the retail operations system.
The `updated_at` column records the timestamp of the most recent update to a store's record and cannot be null.
The "contact_name" column in the "suppliers" table stores the name of the primary contact person for a supplier, allowing null values.
Records the timestamp when a supplier entry is initially created in the system, stored as a non-nullable datetime value.
The "email" column in the "suppliers" table stores the email address of a supplier as a nullable nvarchar value for contact purposes.
The "name" column in the "suppliers" table is a non-nullable nvarchar field intended to store the name of a supplier, though sample data indicates it is currently unused.
The "phone" column in the "suppliers" table stores the contact phone number of a supplier as a nullable string.
Unique identifier for each supplier, serving as the primary key in the suppliers table.
The `updated_at` column stores the non-nullable timestamp of the most recent update to a supplier record in the `suppliers` table.
